content:
# Finite Volume Method

**Summary**: A numerical method for solving partial differential equations by discretizing the domain into small volumes.

---

## How It Is Used in the Thesis Collection

- [[2014-jamil|JAMIL, 2014]] used finite volume method to solve mass, momentum, and energy conservation equations in porous media.

- [[2018-jamal|Jamal, 2018]] used finite volume method to solve the Brinkman's equation and advection-diffusion-adsorption equations.

- [[2022-aboluhom|Aboluhom, 2022]] used the finite volume method to discretize the flow, reaction, and thermal models.

- [[2024-alabdrabalnabi|Alabdrabalnabi, 2024]] used finite volume method (FVM) to solve flow, mass transfer, and heat transfer equations in a 2D and 3D grid structure.

## Instruments and Software



- [[2018-jamal|Jamal, 2018]] used finite volume method to solve the Brinkman's equation and advection-diffusion-adsorption equations.

- [[2022-aboluhom|Aboluhom, 2022]] used the finite volume method to discretize the flow, reaction, and thermal models.

- [[2024-alabdrabalnabi|Alabdrabalnabi, 2024]] used finite volume method (FVM) to solve flow, mass transfer, and heat transfer equations in a 2D and 3D grid structure.

## Typical Workflow



## Key Results and Findings

- The model was validated against experimental data of hydrogen adsorption on activated carbon. ([[2014-jamil|JAMIL, 2014, p. 19]])

- The cell-centered finite volume discretization was used to solve the system of equations for pressure, velocity, and concentration. ([[2018-jamal|Jamal, 2018, p. 48]])

- The flow model was discretized into a form where coefficients are expressed in terms of grid properties. ([[2022-aboluhom|Aboluhom, 2022, p. 45]])

- The FVM allowed for the calculation of pressure profiles from which velocity, concentration, and temperature distributions were derived. ([[2024-alabdrabalnabi|Alabdrabalnabi, 2024, p. 94]])
